data.to: Hepatology Department, Milton Keynes University Hospital
data.from: Dr. C. Evans, Newport Pagnell Surgery
data.reason_for_referral: Significantly elevated transaminases found on screening bloods. Patient reports occasional fatigue but is otherwise generally well. High alcohol consumption reported.
data.alcohol_intake: Reports approximately 50 units per week. Mostly spirits. Drinking heavily since university.

data.chief_complaint: Came in for routine health check requested by employer. Reports occasional tiredness but attributes to work stress. Otherwise well.

physical_examination.skin: No jaundice. No spider naevi. No palmar erythema.

data.assessment: Markedly elevated transaminases (ALT 550, AST 480) with normal ALP. Hepatocellular pattern. Very high alcohol intake is the likely aetiology. Needs hepatology input and alcohol reduction advice.

sections.title: Pre-Consultation Questionnaire
qa: About 50 units per week. Mostly vodka and whisky. Been drinking like this since uni (about 8 years).
Thiamine, multivitamin (started recently on GP advice)
